title: APEX Module Manifest
description: Manifest contract for an APEX module under framework/modules/<name>/manifest.json. Manifest-driven plugin model: each module is independently versioned, owned, and discoverable. Submodules (separate git repos) are a future migration path; the manifest is the architectural commitment for R5.

name.description: Canonical module name. Convention: 'apex-<domain>' for domain modules, 'apex-core' for the core module.

owner.description: Free-text owner — usually the org / team / handle responsible for the module.

status.description: active = module is implemented and dispatchable; stub = scaffold only, not yet implemented; core = the meta-module that defines what 'core' means.

capabilities.description: Free-form capability tags advertised by this module (e.g., 'data', 'frontend', 'security', 'test-architecture').

agent_path.description: Path to the agent prompt body, relative to the manifest directory. Convention: 'agent.md'. Null for stubs and for apex-core.

hooks.description: List of hook contributions provided by this module. Each hook references a path under framework/hooks/ (the live tree remains flat; modules contribute, they do not host).

deps.description: Other module names this module depends on. Resolved via _registry.json.

dispatch_aliases.description: Optional alias names a dispatcher Task("<name>", ...) call may resolve to this module's agent. Lets the manifest stay aligned with the agent's frontmatter `name` field while documenting compatibility names.

notes.description: Free-form notes for human readers (status caveats, migration history, etc.).
